Title: Successful high-level accumulation of fish oil omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acids in a transgenic oilseed crop

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil; TAG, DAG, PC, PE and other seed lipid classes
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Δ6-desaturation entry step for LC-PUFA engineering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: linoleic acid (18:2, LA); alpha-linolenic acid (18:3, ALA)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: gamma-linolenic acid (18:3, GLA); stearidonic acid (18:4, SDA)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ6 double bond into C18 PUFA substrates, converting LA to GLA and ALA to SDA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream precursors for EPA and DHA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables flux into engineered LC-PUFA pathway and supports high EPA/DHA accumulation with only minor accumulation of C18 Δ6-desaturated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within multigene constructs; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: marked decrease in oleic acid in transgenic seeds, reflecting diversion of flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OtΔ6 was included as a core catalytic entry enzyme in both p5_EPA and p7_DHA constructs. 2. Its expression enabled efficient Δ6 desaturation with only low accumulation of GLA and SDA intermediates. 3. This helped avoid undesirable intermediate build-up while supporting fish-oil-like EPA/DHA levels.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for engineering seed oils enriched in omega-3 LC-PUFAs; useful in stacked pathways when paired with efficient downstream elongase/desaturases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights previous observation that expression of O. tauri Δ6-desaturase in transgenic plants dramatically decreases oleic acid.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates Δ6-desaturated C18 fatty acids to the corresponding C20 products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors of ARA/EPA and supports DHA route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports efficient conversion of C18 Δ6 products into C20 intermediates, contributing to high EPA accumulation and low undesirable C18 intermediate levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within multigene constructs; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as an efficiency-critical step by comparison with prior inefficient systems, but not directly established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSE1 was part of both EPA- and DHA-producing constructs. 2. Efficient elongation minimized accumulation of GLA/SDA compared with previous plant systems with poor Δ6 elongation. 3. This contributed to successful high EPA production in Camelina seed oil.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important component for avoiding pathway bottlenecks and intermediate accumulation in omega-3 seed-oil engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper contrasts its performance with previous Brassica carinata work compromised by inefficient Δ6 elongation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ5 double bond into C20 PUFA substrates to generate EPA from ETA and ARA from DGLA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product EPA and precursor ARA for omega-3 conversion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly responsible for EPA synthesis in transgenic Camelina seeds and contributes to high EPA accumulation with relatively low omega-6 intermediate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within multigene constructs; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key catalytic step for direct EPA formation, but rate limitation not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcΔ5 was essential for EPA production in both constructs. 2. Expression supported accumulation of EPA up to fish-oil-comparable levels in Camelina seed. 3. Omega-3 products greatly exceeded omega-6 products, giving a favorable n-3/n-6 ratio.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential direct EPA-forming enzyme for transgenic oilseed production of omega-3 LC-PUFAs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EPA accumulation reached up to 31% in individual RRes_EPA seeds and remained substantial in RRes_DHA seeds.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ12 double bond into oleic acid to produce linoleic acid, enhancing LA-CoA substrate supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts LA substrate availability for the Δ6-desaturase step, supporting overall EPA/DHA pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within multigene constructs; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated with reduced oleic acid because OA is converted and overall pathway flux is diverted
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsΔ12 was included to enhance LA-CoA levels for pathway entry. 2. It functioned as a precursor-supply module in the multigene EPA/DHA constructs. 3. The engineered lines showed major OA reduction consistent with redirected flux into PUFA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful support gene to increase precursor availability in stacked LC-PUFA engineering strategies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states PsΔ12 was included to enhance levels of LA-CoA as substrate for OtΔ6.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts omega-6 long-chain PUFA substrates such as ARA into the corresponding omega-3 products such as EPA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product EPA and improves omega-3/omega-6 balance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances conversion of omega-6 LC-PUFAs to omega-3 products, contributing to high EPA accumulation and favorable n-3/n-6 ratio
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within multigene constructs; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pi-ω3 was used to increase conversion of ARA to EPA. 2. Transgenic Camelina accumulated much higher omega-3 than omega-6 fatty acids, with n-3/n-6 around 9:1. 3. This helped minimize undesirable omega-6 intermediate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable branch-conversion enzyme to maximize omega-3 purity and nutritional quality in engineered oils
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text refers to x3-desaturase; functionally this is an ω3-desaturase improving EPA production from omega-6 LC-PUFA intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates C20 LC-PUFAs such as EPA to C22 products such as DPA in the DHA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the elongation step required for conversion of EPA toward DHA in the p7_DHA construct, enabling simultaneous EPA and DHA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within p7_DHA construct; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream step for DHA pathway, not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OtElo5 was added to p5_EPA to build the seven-gene DHA pathway. 2. Its inclusion enabled conversion of EPA toward C22 intermediates needed for DHA synthesis. 3. The resulting lines accumulated DHA up to 14% in individual seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential elongase for extending EPA to DHA pathway in oilseed metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Added to p5_EPA together with EhΔ4 to create p7_DHA.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary health-beneficial omega-3 LC-PUFA; sustainable alternative to fish oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil; especially polar lipids and TAG in RRes_DHA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ4 double bond into DPA to generate DHA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables DHA biosynthesis in Camelina seeds, producing mean 8% DHA in RRes_DHA and up to 14% DHA in individual seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina within p7_DHA construct; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS and lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: DHA showed less efficient flux into TAG than EPA and stronger retention in PC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EhΔ4 was the terminal DHA-forming enzyme added in the seven-gene construct. 2. Its expression enabled substantial DHA accumulation in Camelina seed oil alongside EPA. 3. DHA partitioning differed from EPA, with stronger PC bias and less efficient TAG flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical terminal enzyme for producing fish-oil-like DHA in engineered oilseed crops; may need co-engineering of host lipid flux for better TAG deposition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positional analysis supports that DHA is synthesized on sn-2 PC and then channelled less efficiently than EPA into TAG.
